frameworks.name: cross-codal integration in narrative comprehension
frameworks.one_line: comprehenders integrate event information across pictorial and textual codalities

frameworks.name: visual narrative event processing
frameworks.one_line: narratives convey event sequences via different code systems

frameworks.name: bridging-event inference
frameworks.one_line: missing intermediate events are inferred to link panels into coherent sequences

core_question: How does the codality (picture vs text vs blank) of bridging-event information affect comprehension of visual narrative events?
key_assumptions: narrative comprehension requires integrating across panels
viewing time on end-state panels indexes integration cost
pictures and text differ as event-information codalities
manipulations: codality of the bridging panel: picture, text, or absent
absent vs blank-panel replacement of the bridging event
direct comparison of text, picture, and blank conditions
key_findings: end-state viewing times comparable between absent and text bridging conditions
end-state viewing times longer in text than pictorial bridging condition
end-state viewing times longest when bridging panel was a blank
blank-panel distortion exceeds the cost of cross-codal integration
interpretations: cross-codal integration during narrative comprehension is possible but incurs additional mental effort
